description: CISSP Trap Framework - Metadata Reference for Student Reports

MGT.name: Managerial Mindset
MGT.red_flag: You picked 'install X' instead of 'draft policy'
MGT.study_focus: Governance frameworks, policy creation, business continuity
MGT.report_note: You picked a technical solution when the question required strategic/managerial thinking

MOD.name: Negative/Modifier
MOD.red_flag: You read 'LEAST effective' and picked the MOST effective
MOD.study_focus: Read question stem twice, highlight key words
MOD.report_note: You missed a critical keyword (NOT/LEAST/EXCEPT/MOST) in the question

ABS.name: Absolute Language
ABS.red_flag: You picked an answer saying 'completely eliminate risk'
ABS.study_focus: Residual risk ALWAYS exists in security
ABS.report_note: Absolute language is usually a trap - security has residual risk

FAM.name: False Assumption/Vendor Jargon
FAM.red_flag: You picked 'Palo Alto firewall' instead of 'NGFW'
FAM.study_focus: Use ISC2 glossary terms, not vendor brands
FAM.report_note: The question tests knowledge of standard terminology, not specific brands

BEST.name: Best/Primary/Most
BEST.description: Distinguish most effective from acceptable answers
BEST.study_focus: Rank all options by quality, always pick the highest
BEST.report_note: You picked a correct answer, but a better/more strategic one existed

ALL.name: All-of-the-Above / Umbrella Effect
ALL.description: All answers are true - pick the broadest umbrella answer
ALL.red_flag: You picked partial answer when broader option encompassed it
ALL.study_focus: Look for 'Defense in Depth', 'Risk Management', 'holistic' answers
ALL.report_note: You picked an answer that's technically correct but not the broadest/best option

NON.name: Non-sequitur / Shiny Object
NON.description: Technically correct but answers the wrong question
NON.study_focus: Read question stem 3 times, identify what it's really asking
NON.report_note: You picked a technically correct answer that doesn't address the question asked

TIM.name: Timeline Trick
TIM.red_flag: You picked 'update training' instead of 'disconnect network' for immediate response
TIM.report_note: For immediate/first response questions, prioritize containment over prevention

IRP.name: Incident Response Process
IRP.description: Preparation → Detection → Containment → Eradication → Recovery → Lessons Learned
IRP.red_flag: You picked 'eradicate malware' as the FIRST step instead of 'contain'
IRP.study_focus: Learn NIST IR phases: Detect → Contain → Eradicate → Recover → Lessons
IRP.report_note: You mixed up the order of incident response phases

BCP.name: Business Continuity Plan
BCP.description: Initiation → BIA → Continuity Strategies → Approval → Testing
BCP.red_flag: You picked 'write the plan' first instead of 'conduct BIA'
BCP.study_focus: BIA (Business Impact Analysis) comes BEFORE strategy design
BCP.report_note: You got the order of BCP phases wrong

SDL.name: SDLC Phases
SDL.red_flag: You picked 'fix bug in production' instead of 'use dev environment'
SDL.report_note: You didn't follow proper SDLC phase sequence

FOR.name: Forensics Order
FOR.red_flag: You picked 'shut down system' first instead of 'capture RAM'
FOR.study_focus: Order of volatility: RAM → Disk → Network
FOR.report_note: You didn't follow proper order of volatility for evidence capture

ORD.name: Process Sequence
ORD.red_flag: You mixed up phases or steps of a sequential process
ORD.study_focus: Identify the process, memorize the correct order
ORD.report_note: You got the sequence of process steps wrong

SCO.name: Scope & Boundaries
SCO.description: Cloud Consumer vs. Cloud Provider responsibilities (CCSP)
SCO.red_flag: You forgot encryption is Consumer's job in IaaS but Provider's in SaaS
SCO.study_focus: CSA Cloud Responsibility Matrix - memorize by service model
SCO.report_note: You confused Cloud Consumer vs. Provider responsibilities

RAC.name: Role Confusion
RAC.description: Data Owner vs. Data Custodian vs. System Admin
RAC.red_flag: You picked 'IT Admin' instead of 'Business Manager' for authorization
RAC.report_note: You confused who decides vs. who implements

GEO.name: Jurisdiction
GEO.red_flag: You picked technical control instead of 'consult Legal'
GEO.report_note: You didn't recognize that legal/compliance requirements override technical fixes

ETH.name: Ethical Fence
ETH.description: Legal vs. Ethical vs. Practical - ISC2 Code of Ethics first
ETH.red_flag: You picked 'hack back' instead of 'protect society first'
ETH.study_focus: ISC2 Ethics: Protect society > Follow law > Protect employer
ETH.report_note: You didn't apply ISC2 Code of Ethics principle

RFP.name: Risk Framework Puzzle
RFP.description: Accept vs. Mitigate vs. Transfer vs. Avoid
RFP.red_flag: You picked 'implement firewall' (Mitigate) instead of 'buy insurance' (Transfer)
RFP.study_focus: Know when each risk response is appropriate
RFP.report_note: You picked the wrong risk response strategy

SLA.name: Service Level Agreement
SLA.description: Reactive vs. Proactive - RTO/RPO driven decisions
SLA.red_flag: You picked 'restore server' instead of 'activate DR plan' per RTO/RPO
SLA.study_focus: SLA/RTO/RPO dictate what action to take, not your gut
SLA.report_note: You didn't consider RTO/RPO requirements in your decision

CUL.name: Culture Shock
CUL.description: User training vs. technical control - people > technology
CUL.red_flag: You picked 'DLP software' instead of 'awareness training'
CUL.study_focus: Root cause is usually human behavior, not technology
CUL.report_note: You picked a technical solution when training/awareness was the real fix

DEF_DIST.name: Definition + Distinction
DEF_DIST.description: Know what X means AND how it differs from similar terms
DEF_DIST.example: Q4: MAD vs RTO vs RPO
DEF_DIST.study_strategy: Create comparison matrices and Venn diagrams

DEFINITION.name: Pure Definition
DEFINITION.description: Know the exact, official definition of a term
DEFINITION.example: Q7: Policy is promulgated by senior management

PURPOSE.name: Purpose/Use Case
PURPOSE.description: Know WHEN and WHERE something should be used
PURPOSE.example: Q17: SLA for regularly recurring activities
PURPOSE.study_strategy: Study use cases, context, and appropriate applications

APPLICATION.name: Scenario Application
APPLICATION.example: Long scenario questions with organizational context
APPLICATION.study_strategy: Practice case studies, think like a CISO

COMPARISON.name: Compare/Distinguish
COMPARISON.description: Identify differences and relationships between concepts
COMPARISON.study_strategy: Create Venn diagrams and comparison charts

CALCULATION.name: Math/Metrics
CALCULATION.description: Compute specific values, formulas, or percentages
CALCULATION.example: Calculate ALE, RTO, or risk scores

ACRONYM.name: Multiple Acronyms
ACRONYM.description: Distinguish between similar or related acronyms
ACRONYM.example: GDPR vs CCPA vs HIPAA vs PCI-DSS
ACRONYM.study_strategy: Create acronym decoder sheets, use color coding

FRAMEWORK.name: Framework/Model Knowledge
FRAMEWORK.description: Know components, phases, or principles of a framework
FRAMEWORK.example: NIST CSF pillars, SDLC phases, CIA triad

DISTINCTION.name: Semantic Distinction
DISTINCTION.description: Distinguish between similar-sounding or conceptually close terms

CONCEPT_GENERAL.name: General Concept Understanding
CONCEPT_GENERAL.description: Broad concept comprehension without specific categorization

CRITICAL.description: These are objective rules that apply across exams

HIGH.description: Common confusion points that appear frequently

MEDIUM.description: Important but less frequent trap patterns
MEDIUM.action: Study with examples and practice questions

report_mapping.WRONG_with_CRITICAL_trap: High-impact error - student needs targeted review

at_exam: Read LAST SENTENCE of question first (to identify role and action word)
Highlight key words: BEST, FIRST, LEAST, NOT, EXCEPT, MOST
Eliminate answers with absolute language (always, never, all, completely)
If stem says 'manager/policy', eliminate technical answers
If multiple answers look right, pick the broadest/umbrella answer
Match the role in the stem (analyst→technical, manager→strategy)
